items.q: What drives the movement of tectonic plates?
items.correct: Convection currents in the mantle combined with slab pull at subduction zones and ridge push at constructive boundaries.
items.misconception: The Earth's rotation makes the plates move.
items.random_wrong: Plates move because of ocean tides pulling on the crust.
items.why: Students conflate the Earth spinning with crustal motion. Plate motion is driven by heat-driven convection in the mantle, not rotation — rotation only affects things like the Coriolis effect.

items.q: Why do some rivers form deltas and others form estuaries?
items.correct: Deltas form where rivers deposit sediment faster than the sea can remove it (calm seas, heavy load); estuaries form where tidal energy removes sediment (strong tides).
items.misconception: Deltas form on big rivers and estuaries on small ones.
items.random_wrong: Deltas form in cold climates and estuaries in warm climates.
items.why: Size doesn't determine the form. The balance of sediment supply vs marine energy does — the Nile (delta) and Thames (estuary) are similar size but in different sea conditions.

items.q: What are the three cells of global atmospheric circulation?
items.correct: The Hadley cell (equator to 30 degrees), Ferrel cell (30 to 60 degrees) and Polar cell (60 degrees to poles) together redistribute heat from equator to poles.
items.misconception: There is one big Hadley cell from the equator to each pole.
items.random_wrong: The three cells are tropical, temperate and arctic, named after climate zones.
items.why: Students often learn 'Hadley' as the only cell. There are three in each hemisphere, and their boundaries (at 30 and 60 degrees) explain global pressure belts, deserts and the polar front.
Correct answer: 2 million m^3/yr. “200000 m^3/yr” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A spit is best classified as a:
items.why: Correct answer: Sink. “Source” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Spurn Head spit length in km:
items.why: The real figure is 5.5. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
